name: Winter Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 quarts chicken or vegetable stock (12 cups)
1 pound fresh mushrooms, wiped clean and thinly sliced
White part only from 2 large leeks, halved lengthwise then cut into 1.5-in. lengths
1/2 pound firm potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
This is mushroom-barley soup at its best. The recipe is spicy and rich.
Dried mushrooms are soaked in stock and the soaking liquid is strained through a coffee filter to remove any particles. If you have leftover soup, you'll need a bit more stock to thin it for serving the next time.
1. In a small saucepan, bring 1.5 cups stock just to boil. Place dried mushrooms in a small bowl and pour steaming stock over them. Soak mushrooms for 15 minutes. Drain mushrooms, squeezing out excess liquid and reserving all liquid. Strain the mushroom broth through a paper coffee filter rinsed with hot water. Reserve strained broth. Chop soaked mushrooms and reserve.
2. In a large soup pot over low heat, melt butter. Stir in fresh mushrooms and cook until they soften and release moisture, about 10 minutes. Stir in the remaining 9.5 cups stock, the soaked mushrooms and their broth, and the onion, leeks, carrots, parsnips, garlic, and bay leaves. Increase heat and bring mixture to boil. Skim surface if necessary. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. Stir in barley and simmer for 20 minutes. Stir in potatoes and simmer until barley and potatoes are tender, about 30 minutes. Remove bay leaves. Remove soup from heat and season with salt, pepper, and dill. Ladle soup into individual-serving bowls and swirl a spoonful of sour cream into each portion. Makes 3 quarts; 8 to 10 servings.
Each serving contains approximately 1 vegetable exchange, 2 bread/starch exchanges, 1 fat exchange; 190 calories, 33 gm. carbohydrate, 8 gm. protein, 4 gm. fat (including 2 gm. saturated fat), 6 mg. cholesterol, 555 mg. sodium, 54 mg. calcium and 6 gm. dietary fiber.
